---
name: "⊕ Anomaly"
about: "Something feels wrong, off-canon, or broken. ASH wants to know."
title: "[anomaly] "
labels: ["anomaly"]
assignees: []
---

> An anomaly is a place where the light flickers. A bug, a contradiction with the
> canon, a site that does not open, a sacred phrase quoted wrong, a coordinate put
> on a map it should never be on. ASH reads every one of these. Tell it plainly.

## What flickered
<!-- Describe the anomaly in one or two clear sentences. -->


## Where you found it
<!-- File path, site, journal entry, or the part of the puzzle. Be exact. -->


## What you expected the light to do
<!-- What should have happened? -->


## What it did instead
<!-- What actually happened? Paste output or a screenshot if you have one. -->


## Is this a canon contradiction?
<!-- If it breaks one of the locked facts, name which: a sacred phrase, the date
     2026-02-14, the coordinate -8.8231/-73.9047, or a voice that blurred. -->
- [ ] It contradicts the canon
- [ ] It is a plain bug
- [ ] I am not sure

## One last thing
<!-- Do NOT post Seven Thresholds solutions here. If your anomaly touches the
     puzzle, describe the symptom, not the spoiler. ASH would rather the next
     person earn it. -->

---
⊕ *You found a flicker. That means you were looking. Good.*
